footer.tagline: The first health and wellness app for young female athletes.
footer.ctaText: Join the waitlist and become part of the community breaking barriers.

home.badge: Breaking All Barriers for the Next Gen Athletes
home.heroTitle: One in two girls drops out of sport with puberty because she feels wrong.
home.heroBody: BAB helps adolescent athletes recognize, understand and manage their body's signals precisely when it becomes most unpredictable, so they keep playing and having fun — performing at their best and feeling their best.
home.coachDesc: Help your athletes understand their body's signals and stay in sport, with secure, aggregated data.
home.parentDesc: Support your daughter through puberty changes, ensuring total privacy and healthy sports.
home.bivioSub: BAB adapts to your role. Choose the right path for you.
home.bivioCoachDesc: Access readiness indexes in real-time. Intervene before injuries happen, without ever exposing individual biological data.
home.bivioParentDesc: Receive practical advice on sleep and nutrition. Without ever seeing your daughter's biological data. Her privacy is absolute.
home.dataTitle:
Women are treated as miniature men,
and girls as small women.
home.data1Desc: Believe their menstrual cycle negatively affects athletic performance
home.data2Desc: Of all sports research is conducted exclusively on female athletes
home.data3Desc: Scientific research on injuries in adolescent female athletes
home.testimonialsTitle: What Our Community Says About What BAB Is Building
home.missionTitle: BAB is your coach off the pitch.
home.missionBody: With quizzes, games and sporting metaphors, it supports girls' growth in sport — teaching them to track, listen to and communicate how they feel to teammates, coaches and parents.
home.missionBodyHighlight: So they face puberty without feeling wrong or alone on their journey.
home.silenceLead: The same voice, before and after BAB.
silenceOldVoices: I keep it all to myself
I'm scared I'm not good anymore
silenceBabVoices: I know and value my body
I work with my body, not against it
I don't have to do it alone
I play better because I know myself better
I can explain what I need
home.silenceFootnote: Real statements from 25 qualitative interviews with 13–14 y.o. athletes.
home.marquee: 🕵️‍♀️ GDPR Compliant ✦ 🧪 Backed by Science ✦ 👟 Built with Elite Athletes ✦ ✨ Puberty Support ✦
home.footerTitle:
Reach Your Personal Best,
By Feeling Your Best.
home.privacy1: Only if you give consent, the data you track contributes to anonymous scientific research aimed at improving the health, wellbeing, and performance of both you and other young athletes. Together, we're helping close the gaps in youth and sports research.
home.privacy2: Your data is always anonymous, protected, and used solely to advance teenagers' health, wellbeing, development, and performance.
home.heroPhotoAlt: Teenage athlete during training, black-and-white portrait
home.dataPhotoAlt: Portrait of a BAB athlete next to data on girls dropping out of sport
home.dataSources: Sources: ① Jones, Bishop et al. (2024), 128 elite athletes, 76.8% (PMC10912517). ② Cowley et al. (2021), “Invisible Sportswomen”, WSPAJ 29(2): 6% of studies on women only. ③ Horan et al. (2024), Sports Medicine: just 32 injury studies on under-19 female athletes. Full references on request.

coach.accessibleNote: Aggregated team data · athletes' privacy always protected
coach.headline: Help your athletes stay in sport — and your club keep them.
coach.sub: One in two girls drops out of sport during puberty. BAB gives your athletes the tools to listen to their bodies and stay on the pitch. You only receive aggregated, anonymous team signals — never the cycle or individual health.
coach.value1Desc: See when the team is struggling and step in before girls quit: fewer drop-outs, a stronger squad.
coach.value2Desc: Aggregated fatigue signals help you balance load and recovery at the right time, before overload becomes a problem.
coach.value3Desc: Only aggregated, anonymous data, compliant with GDPR for minors. The cycle and individual health are never visible: families trust you, and you’re protected.
coach.pricingValue: Custom pricing based on the number of teams
coach.privacyInline: You see only aggregated, anonymous data. Never individual biological data.
coach.signal1Desc: Above the group average this week. Consider a lighter session.
coach.signal1Cta: Send a note to the team
coach.signal2Desc: They asked how to manage energy and training on tougher days.
coach.how1Desc: From the free app they log energy, mood and body signals, privately.
coach.how2Desc: Only aggregated, anonymous data: participation, average energy and group flags.
coach.how3Desc: Adjust load, send a note or answer the team’s questions — at the right time.
coach.ctaReassure: Free demo, no commitment. We’ll get back to you within a few days.

about.missionLead: We can no longer accept that female athletes are treated as "small men". We're building tomorrow's standard, combining science, biological data and empathetic technology.
about.story1: At thirteen, Mia's body no longer felt like home. She had grown taller in a matter of months, and before she understood what was happening, hormones had changed more than just her body. They changed how she played and how she felt while doing it.
about.story2pre: Pushing through period symptoms, fatigue and hunger, she learned that
about.story2post: . Disconnecting from her body became her winning strategy.
about.story3: Soon, injuries and anxiety started creeping in and her performance began to drop. And because no one talked about any of it, Mia kept quiet and started questioning whether she was no longer made for sport.
about.story4pre: But it wasn't Mia who had fallen behind.
about.story4highlight: It was sport that failed her
about.story4post: by neglecting her changing body, health and wellbeing.
about.bigCaption: Just like Mia, several athletes pay the cost of bleeding in sport.

stats.label: Believe their menstrual cycle negatively affects performance
Drop out of sport during puberty
Of sports research is conducted on female athletes only
Scientific research on injuries in adolescent female athletes

about.founderBadge2: Former Marketing Specialist @ Adidas (~5 yrs) & Puma (2.5 yrs)
about.founderQuote1: "I started from a personal frustration: after a career in competitive sport, I experienced drop-out first-hand because the system didn't know how to handle the changes in my body."
about.founderQuote2: "After years working in marketing for giants like Adidas and Puma, studying sport's impact on communities and the barriers young athletes face, I realised the scientific and communication gap for girls under 18 is structural."
about.founderQuote3: "BAB is the technological shield I wish I'd had myself, so I wouldn't feel wrong on the field."
about.photoAlt: Young athlete from the BAB community
about.statsSources: Sources: Jones & Bishop (2024, n=128); ~1 in 2 drop out at puberty; Cowley et al. (2021), “Invisible Sportswomen”; Horan et al. (2024). Full references on request.

simulator.title: Simulator
simulator.heading: Try it yourself
simulator.intro: Tap A, B or C: the avatar reacts to the body's signals — energy, heart rate, recovery. This is how BAB makes what an athlete feels readable, day by day.

items.a: We believe teenage girls shouldn't have to face everything on their own. That's why BAB gives parents, coaches and clubs the right tools and resources to understand and best support young athletes throughout their journey.
BAB is designed primarily to support girls and young women who menstruate and play sport.

items.q: What features does the app include?
items.a: BAB includes cycle, energy and mood tracking, helping athletes understand how these factors affect both sports performance and daily life. The app also offers personalised insights and educational resources developed by our medical team, plus a private, anonymous Q&A where athletes and parents can get answers from health professionals and elite athletes.

items.q: Is BAB safe, anonymous and private?
items.a: Absolutely. BAB is fully compliant with European GDPR regulations. We are committed to keeping your data safe, protected, private and anonymous. All content is reviewed by doctors and sports professionals to ensure it is age-appropriate, evidence-based and safe.

BAB was founded by Gaia Manzone in collaboration with a multidisciplinary team of sports researchers, doctors, elite athletes and coaches, united by a passion for improving the health, wellbeing and performance of young athletes.

lisa.quote: BAB made me feel seen and realise how much I could have benefitted if something like existed during my upbringing in sport. It took me many years to understand that strength is not the opposite of femininity, and the next generation of female athletes should never have to choose between becoming powerful and fitting in.

alisha.quote: Young girls face misinformation and scientific bias, challenges that are often amplified in sports settings, particularly during the profound physical and psychological changes of puberty. BAB provides a safe, preventive solution while contributing to the advancement of research in a field that has been overlooked for far too long.

vasundhara.quote: My daughter is 11 and competes at regional and national levels in fencing. A tool like BAB helps me understand how to best support her athletic goals in a safe and informed way, particularly as she navigates the changes of adolescence while continuing to pursue high-level competition.

erica.quote: As a professional volleyball player and coach, I never had such a great resource as BAB to support the specific needs my teenage athletes and I have.

m1.title: TRACK
m1.desc: Monitor energy levels, sleep and mood, underpinned by menstrual health and growth metrics to build a complete picture of the athlete's wellbeing through longitudinal inputs.

m2.title: UNDERSTAND
m2.desc: Turn the data into clear, simple and engaging language, with explanations of your own data and body-education resources, session after session.

m3.title: OPTIMISE
m3.desc: Puts physical and emotional wellbeing first, and builds performance from there: personalised daily recommendations, defined by clinical experts.

m4.title: PREVENT
m4.desc: Helps you recognise your body’s signals early, with educational reminders and prompts, before they can affect health, wellbeing or performance.

m5.title: SUPPORT
m5.desc: Create a stronger support system around the athlete both on and off the field with dedicated tools for parents and coaches.

faqs.q1: How does biological data privacy work?
faqs.a1: All sensitive data, including menstrual cycle data, is encrypted and stored locally on the athlete's phone. The coach only sees anonymous aggregates or generic alerts (e.g., 'Rest recommended').
faqs.q2: Is it suitable for all sports?
faqs.a2: Yes, BAB adapts to the workloads of every sport, whether team or individual.
faqs.q3: Is the app free for athletes?
faqs.a3: Yes, the mobile app for athletes is completely free. Sports clubs subscribe for access to the Coach Dashboard.

legal.contact: For any question about how we handle your data, or to exercise your rights, write to us at bab.community.official@gmail.com.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: BAB (Breaking All Barriers) cares about the privacy of athletes and the people who support them. This notice explains what data we collect through this site, why, and what rights you have.

sections.p: The data controller is BAB — Breaking All Barriers. You can reach us at any time at bab.community.official@gmail.com.

We only collect the data you give us voluntarily through the site forms: your email address and, optionally, your sport, area of interest, role, club name and the message you send in a demo request. We do not collect individual health data through this site.

We process your data on the basis of your consent (Art. 6.1.a GDPR) to contact you about the waitlist, demos and BAB updates. Site-usage analytics are aggregated and anonymous, based on our legitimate interest in improving the service.

The forms on this site are intended for adults (coaches, club managers, parents). Where processing concerns underage athletes, it only happens with the consent of a parent or guardian. If you believe a minor has given us data without such consent, contact us and we will delete it.
Leads are stored securely via Supabase, our infrastructure provider. We do not sell or share your data with third parties for marketing purposes.
We keep data only as long as needed for validation and contact, and in any case until you withdraw consent. After that it is deleted or anonymised.

You have the right to access, rectify, erase, restrict or object to the processing of your data and to data portability. To exercise these rights or withdraw consent, write to bab.community.official@gmail.com. You also have the right to lodge a complaint with your data protection authority.
We may update this notice over time. The version in force is always the one published on this page, with the update date shown at the top.

cookie.title: Cookie Policy
cookie.intro: This page explains how BAB uses cookies and similar technologies on this site.

sections.p: Cookies are small text files a site saves on your device. They help the site work and, in some cases, measure how it is used.
We use technical local storage to remember your language preference and your cookie consent. These are necessary for the site to work and do not require consent.
If enabled, we may use Google Analytics 4 and Meta Pixel to understand how the site is used and improve campaigns. These tools load only after your explicit consent via the banner.

You can accept or reject non-essential cookies from the banner shown on your first visit. You can change your mind at any time by clearing cookies and site data in your browser: the banner will reappear.

terms.title: Terms & Conditions
terms.intro: By using this site you accept the following terms. Please read them carefully.

sections.p: This site presents BAB — Breaking All Barriers and collects expressions of interest (waitlist and demo requests). It does not constitute a binding commercial offer.
Joining the waitlist or submitting a demo request does not constitute a contract nor guarantee access to the product. We will contact you with details when the service becomes available.
All site content (text, graphics, logo, code) belongs to BAB and may not be used or reproduced without written permission.
The site is provided "as is". The information here is for general purposes and does not replace the advice of a doctor or qualified health professional.
These terms are governed by Italian law. Any dispute falls under the jurisdiction of the controller's registered location.

cookieBanner.text: We use technical cookies and, only with your consent, analytics cookies to improve the site.
